cluster_name: Multiple UK defence ministers resign over inadequate Defence Investment Plan funding
event_summary: UK Defence Secretary John Healey resigned on 11 June 2026 over the Defence Investment Plan funding shortfall; Armed Forces Minister Al Carns followed hours later, and Dan Jarvis was named as the new Defence Secretary.
tldr: Headlines across 13 press ecosystems converge on two claims: the resignation was primarily a political blow to Starmer ('shaken to core', 'seismic crisis', 'sous pression') and the spending plan is materially inadequate ('poche risorse', 'low funding', 'underfunded and outdated'). Russia's RT uniquely diminishes the crisis to a 'spat' — the only headline in the corpus to use a dismissive register — while Fox News is the sole outlet to place the resignation explicitly within the Trump-era NATO pressure campaign.

evidence.quote: As crises mount, how long can UK's Starmer survive?
PM shaken to core after two defence ministers quit
Starmer in 'seismic' crisis, UK defense chief quits
UK defense secretary quits amid spat with Starmer
si dimette, 'poche risorse per forze armate'
U.K. Defence Secretary John Healey quits over low funding
UK's defence plan is underfunded and outdated
Starmer's spending plans leave Britain less safe
Defence Plan Falls Short Amid Rising Global Threats

a.quote: UK defense secretary quits amid spat with Starmer

b.quote: John Healey Announces Shock Resignation in Funding Row

paradox.joint_conclusion: Russia's RT frames the resignation as a 'spat' — a dismissive, trivialising word — while Ukraine's Kyiv Post leads with 'Shock Resignation', maximising the severity. Both note the funding angle, but opposite registers ('spat' vs 'shock') betray diametrically opposed strategic interests: Moscow signals British institutional dysfunction while Kyiv alarms readers that a key military ally is weakening.

single_outlet_findings.finding: Only headline to name Trump and the NATO summit, reframing a UK domestic political crisis through a Washington geopolitical lens invisible in every other outlet's title.
Only outlet to use the word 'spat' in its headline, uniquely minimising what every other corpus title treats as a major political or security crisis.
Only headline to use the idiom 'falls on his sword', framing the resignation as honourable self-sacrifice rather than political rupture — a register absent from all 23 other titles.
Only headline to quote Al Carns calling for a 'new way of governing', surfacing a Labour leadership challenge dimension from the title alone.

bottom_line: Headline coverage globally frames the Healey resignation as both a political survival crisis for Starmer and a concrete defence funding failure, with near-universal convergence on the inadequacy framing across Western and non-Western press; Russia's RT stands alone in dismissing the event as a mere 'spat', while Fox News is the sole outlet to embed the story in the Trump-NATO pressure narrative from the headline itself.
